behavior_summary: When the BLANKETS item is examined (U8P_BLANKETS::look), the script chooses a short description/bark based on the item's visual frame and displays it to the player.

The U8P_BLANKETS::look handler compares Item::getFrame(this) against at least three frame values (0x00, 0x01, 0x02) and in each shown branch emits the short bark text "blanket " then issues a suspend to pause the U8P_BLANKETS::look interaction.
No global flags are read or written and no menus or player-choice branching are present; behavior is entirely local to the item instance.
notable_control_flow: U8P_BLANKETS::look branches on Item::getFrame(this) == 0x00 / 0x01 / 0x02 to select which bark branch to run.
Each branch contains a Bark (display short text) followed by suspend — inferred to pause/terminate the U8P_BLANKETS::look display until player continues.
Although branches are frame-specific, the visible output in the excerpt is identical across the three frames; branching appears prepared for per-frame variation.
open_questions: Why do all three shown frame-branches produce the identical bark text "blanket "? (Possibly placeholder data or other frames not shown.)
Are there additional frames or branches outside this excerpt that produce different descriptions?
Does the suspend node end the U8P_BLANKETS::look process or simply pause it until player acknowledgement in the engine's UI semantics (engine-dependent)?

main_function.name: U8P_BLANKETS::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description/bark when the player examines the BLANKETS item; select text based on the item's frame and pause the interaction after displaying it.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player uses the 'U8P_BLANKETS::look' / examine action on the BLANKETS item (processType 0x0208).
Runs in response to an examine/inspect trigger for the item instance (implicit 'this' reference).

function_map.name: U8P_BLANKETS::look
function_map.role: Handle the item's U8P_BLANKETS::look/examine event and show a short bark based on Item::getFrame(this).

external_calls.purpose: Query the BLANKETS item instance's current visual frame/index to decide which description branch to run.
Engine/dialog action to present the short object label/text ('blanket ') to the player.
Engine control to pause/terminate the U8P_BLANKETS::look process after presenting the bark (inferred from suspend usage in U8P_BLANKETS::look handlers).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: When the BLANKETS item is examined (U8P_BLANKETS::look), the script chooses a short description/bark based on the item's visual frame and displays it to the player.
